title: How to choose between options
body: Start with the option you can realistically maintain week to week. Consistency matters more than intensity. The best plan is the one that fits your real schedule—not an ideal one.

title: Why Remote works
body: Remote coaching works by providing a clear plan, accountability, and ongoing adjustments without requiring in-person sessions. Athletes follow a defined structure, check in regularly, and train independently with guidance.

title: Who Remote is best for
body: Remote works best for athletes with busy or unpredictable schedules who can train independently and stay consistent without live sessions. It’s a strong fit for school, travel, or competing priorities.

title: What progress looks like in Remote
body: Progress in Remote shows up as improved consistency, cleaner fundamentals, and steady improvement over time. We track what’s working, adjust when needed, and keep momentum through repeatable habits.

title: Remote vs in-person (simple rule)
body: Choose Remote if you need flexibility and can follow a plan independently. Choose in-person if you need hands-on coaching or struggle to stay consistent without live sessions.

title: You’re not behind
body: Remote is built for busy seasons. When schedules get full, we simplify the plan and focus on what moves the needle. Progress comes from repeatable effort—not perfect weeks.
